description: Ontology foundation scope definition. Not a classifier. Not live scoring.

classes.definition: A discrete digital evidence object that may be classified for evidence posture, provenance, coherence, and synthetic risk signals.
classes.allowed_use: Classification target for evidence posture, provenance gap, and synthetic risk assessment.
classes.prohibited_use: Subject accusation, guilt inference, truth verdict, fake/real binary output.

classes.definition: A record describing origin, publication path, custody, or attribution signals associated with an evidence artifact.
classes.allowed_use: Source confidence evaluation and provenance examination.
classes.prohibited_use: Labeling a person or institution as deceptive, fraudulent, or guilty.

classes.definition: A structured record of a statement, assertion, or claim embedded in or associated with an evidence artifact.
classes.allowed_use: Contextual mapping and claim-evidence relationship examination.
classes.prohibited_use: Truth verdict on the claim, subject accusation, event occurrence determination.
classes.notes: Claim examination is artifact-bound, not subject-bound.

classes.definition: An observable signal about origin, custody, metadata, source chain, or traceability of an evidence artifact.
classes.allowed_use: Provenance gap identification and source chain examination.
classes.prohibited_use: Proof of forgery, fraud accusation, subject involvement verdict.
classes.notes: Signal applies to artifact path, not subject character.

classes.definition: An observable signal about temporal, geographic, institutional, or situational fit of an evidence artifact.
classes.allowed_use: Contextual stability assessment and contextual uncertainty mapping.
classes.prohibited_use: Determining whether an event occurred, judging subject involvement, institutional bad faith.
classes.notes: Context is part of evidence structure, not subject judgment.

classes.definition: A structural, linguistic, media, temporal, or format-based signal indicating whether an evidence artifact holds together under examination.
classes.allowed_use: Forensic coherence assessment and uncertainty marker identification.
classes.prohibited_use: Absolute detection claim, fake/real determination, truth verdict.
classes.notes: Coherence signals only. Not detection authority.

classes.definition: A bounded indicator that synthetic generation, alteration, imitation, or simulation risk signals are present in an evidence artifact.
classes.allowed_use: Evidence posture risk signal communication and further review recommendation.
classes.prohibited_use: Declaring content fake, certifying authenticity, accusing connected subjects.
classes.notes: Risk indicator on artifact condition only.

classes.definition: A bounded indicator that an evidence artifact aligns with a known deception-pattern category requiring further examination.
classes.allowed_use: Pattern alignment communication within output boundary.
classes.prohibited_use: Subject deception verdict, fraud accusation, guilt by association.

classes.definition: An identifiable person, institution, brand, organization, or event depicted or referenced by an evidence artifact. SubjectReference is metadata for contextual orientation only.
classes.allowed_use: Recording that an artifact depicts or references a subject. Orienting examination scope.
classes.prohibited_use: Classification target, deception verdict, guilt assessment, involvement determination, institutional bad-faith judgment.
classes.notes: Artifact–Subject Separation: SubjectReference is explicitly not a classification target. Evidence about evidence is not evidence about people.

classes.definition: The governed constraint on classification outputs: bounded evidence posture, confidence, uncertainty, and recommended checks without truth verdicts or subject accusation.
classes.allowed_use: Governing all future protocol, tool, and report outputs.
classes.prohibited_use: Expanding outputs to verdicts, scores, or subject judgments.

classes.definition: A planned, bounded classification layer that will combine provenance, coherence, context, and risk signals into an evidence posture assessment for artifacts and evidence chains.
classes.allowed_use: Future taxonomy, standard, and protocol development scope definition.
classes.prohibited_use: Active tool claim, live scoring, truth verdict output, subject classification.
classes.notes: Future bounded classification layer per Sprint 2–4 roadmap. Not an active tool. No live scoring.
